If you book more than 10 days before departure and Ryanair informs us about the verification requirement, we'll always offer you this option. 

If you book later or Ryanair doesn’t let us know in advance and we only find out when we try to check you in, we'll notify you and redirect you to their website. We know this is less convenient than letting us take care of it for you, but you should have enough time to verify directly with them, and we'll give you guidance on how to proceed.

To mitigate cyber risks, you first need to know what they are! Key cyber risks that might affect you include:

1. Man-in-the-middle attacks

Data sent out from your devices can be intercepted. Criminals use techniques, such as spoofing, to create replicas of your email addresses. Their aim? To trick your clients into sending money or information.

The strength of such attacks depends on the criminal’s ability to imitate you – which is another key risk in your role.

Error_Air_VoltageTemp_Body: Make sure the room temperature is between 41-89 °F (5-31 °C), and the voltage is stable. If either is out of range, turn off and unplug the appliance until it's back in range. If that doesn't clear the error, contact customer service. Error code: {0}
Err_AC_Overheat_Body: To protect the appliance, the fan will run on High until the outdoor temperature is less than 45 °C (113 °F).
FanSpeed_Overheat_Note: To prevent overheating, the fan switches to High when the outdoor temperature exceeds 45 °C (113 °F).
FAQ_AC_AutoFan_A: The fan speed adjusts to maintain a target temperature of 24 °C (75 °F).
FAQ_Bogong_Eco_Target_A: The room might be warmer than 24 °C (75 °F). The target temperature will only adjust if the room is cooler than 24 °C (75 °F).
FAQ_CleanMop_A: To prevent mildew, remove and dry the mop after every use. Wash the mop in up to 40 °C (104 °F) water. Air dry the mop to prevent shrinking. Make sure the mop is completely dry before you put it back on the robot.
FAQ_ColdStorage_SpoiledFood_A:
Make sure that:
• Your fridge temperature is 4°C. Higher temperatures shorten shelf life.
• Your fridge isn't overpacked. Leave space between food items and fridge walls so cold air can flow properly.
• Foods are covered and packed properly to avoid cross-contamination.
• Foods are stored in the correct compartments. For example, vegetables in the crisper drawer.
FAQ_ColdStorage_TargetNotReached_A:
The appliance might not reach target temperature because:
1. The door is opened too often.
2. Many new or warm foods have been added to the fridge at once. In this case, you can use the *Boost* function (if applicable).
3. The door isn't closed properly, or the seal is damaged.
4. Room temperature is too low or high. The fridge works at 10°C - 43°C.
FAQ_Hum_FrostCoils_A: If the appliance has been turned on recently, then this is normal. It's caused by refrigerant rushing through the coil, and should disappear within 60 minutes.\n\nIf the frost doesn't disappear, then the room temperature might be too low. Make sure the room is at least 41 °F (5 °C).
FAQ_Hum_RoomNotDry_A:
• The target humidity is too high. Choose a lower target humidity, or Continuous mode.

• The appliance has been recently installed or restarted.

• The grille is blocked or dirty.

• The room temperature is too low. Make sure the room is at least 41 °F (5 °C).
FAQ_Oven_Overcooking_A: The Food Sensor does not consider carryover cooking. After you remove the food from the oven, food temperature continues to rise due to residual heat trapped inside. To avoid overcooking, try removing the food when it’s 2-3°C below the target temperature. Remember that carryover estimates vary based on food type and size, so reaching the perfect result might require research and practice.
FAQ_Sleep_A:
Sleep temporarily adjusts your target temperature over an 8-hour period. The Sleep target is easier for the appliance to maintain, so it uses less energy.
The target gradually adjusts by 2 °C (4 °F) over 1 hour, holds for the duration of Sleep, then returns to your original target.

• In Cool, it increases the target temperature.
• In Heat (if applicable), it decreases the target temperature.
• In Auto (if applicable), it either decreases or increases the temperature, depending on whether the appliance is heating or cooling.
FAQ_Sleep_A_LATAM:
Sleep temporarily adjusts your target temperature over an 8-hour period. The Sleep target is easier for the appliance to maintain, so it uses less energy.

The target gradually adjusts 2° (4 °F) over 1 hour, then holds for 5 hours. Over the last hour, it gradually returns to your original target.
• In Cool, the appliance increases the target.
• In Heat, it decreases the target.
• In Auto, it either increases or decreases based on whether the appliance is heating or cooling.
• In Fan, it decreases the fan speed, to imitate the body's nightly temperature decrease.
FAQ_Sleep_A_SEA: Sleep temporarily adjusts your target temperature over a 7-hour period. The Sleep target is easier for the appliance to maintain, so it uses less energy.\n\nThe target gradually increases by 2 °C (4 °F) over 2 hours, then holds for 5 hours. It then decreases 1 °C (2 °F) and holds until you turn off Sleep.
FAQ_SmartTemp_Telica_A: The target temperature is 22 °C (72 °F), to balance comfort and energy use.
FAQ_WarmAir_A:
Make sure that:

• The target temperature is colder than the room.

• The room is at least 16 °C (60.8 °F).

If both of those are true, then you might need to restart the compressor. To do this, change to another mode, then back to Cool. Wait 4 minutes for the compressor to restart.
Feed_30C_Long: 30° gets things clean, while using up to 40%% less energy to heat the water than 40°. \n\nLower temperatures also help reduce fading.
Feed_CleanMop_Extended_Body:
Remove the mop from the robot as soon as possible after mopping. If you can’t wash it right away, rinse it and let it air dry completely.

Wash the mop in up to 40 °C water. Air dry the mop to prevent shrinking.

Make sure the mop is completely dry before you put it back on the robot.
FilterMaintenance_Azul_Intro: Vacuum the filters every 2 weeks. Clogged filters make the appliance work harder and use more energy. \n\nYou can also wash the filters with dish soap and body-temperature water. Make sure they’re completely dry before you put them back in the appliance.
FilterMaintenance_Azul_Singular_Intro: Vacuum the filter every 2 weeks. A clogged filter makes the appliance work harder and use more energy.\n\nYou can also wash the filter with dish soap and body-temperature water. Make sure it's completely dry before you put it back in the appliance.
FilterMaintenance_Yushan_Intro: Vacuum the filter every month. A clogged filter makes the appliance work harder and use more energy.\n\nYou can also wash the filter with dish soap and body-temperature water. Make sure it's completely dry before you put it back in the appliance.
FoodStorage_ShortTerm_FreshChicken_Body: Make sure your fridge is at 4° or colder, then store on bottom shelf to prevent drippings from contaminating other foods. If sealed, follow the expiration date on the original packaging. Once opened, it typically lasts 1-2 days.

We collect the data that is necessary for managing your loyalty account and sending commercial offers.
The legal basis for this processing is your consent.
Your data may be used to infer and analyze your preferences in order to personalize your experience.
They will be kept for a period of 5 years after your last contact with Leroy Merlin.
Leroy Merlin is a member of the Valiuz alliance and joins forces with other brands to better understand your habits and personalize the offers you receive .
You can object to the analysis of your preferences as well as alliance Valiuz directly in your customer account, in the account management and newsletter and communication tab.

To find out more about the processing of your data by Leroy Merlin: click here

loyalty_legal_notice_data_control_conten:
You keep control of your data.
You can request to access them, correct them or delete them, by going here or by email to dpo@leroymerlin.fr.
At any time, you can modify your consent to receive promotional offers from Leroy Merlin, as well as object to the analysis of your preferences. To do this, go to your customer area, to the account management and newsletter and communication tab.

To find out more about your rights: click here
If there is no response, you can file a complaint with the CNIL.

product_ecotaxes_all_description: The eco-contribution is an amount added to the selling price of the product. This amount is donated to an eco-organisation approved by the State, and makes it possible to finance the collection, recycling and depollution of furniture and electrical and electronic equipment.
product_ecotaxes_d3e_description: Since November 15, 2006, all electrical and electronic products sold to households are subject to the eco-participation WEEE (Waste of Electrical and Electronic Equipment). The eco-contribution is indicated on the labels in a visible way and separated from the price of the product. This funds the collection, recycling and depollution of these products. Its amount varies according to the product and the type of treatment it requires.
product_ecotaxes_d3e_message: of which {0}{1} eco-share for recycling WEEE
product_ecotaxes_dea_description: Since May 1, 2013, all new furniture sold is subject to the eco-participation. Its amount is added to the sale price of the product, and is donated to Eco-mobilier, an eco-organization approved by the State, which aims to collect and recover used furniture by giving it a second life, by recycling it or using it as a source of energy. This amount varies depending on the product and the type of processing it requires.
product_ecotaxes_dea_label: éco-part. in the recycling of furniture
product_ecotaxes_dea_message: of which {0}{1} eco-share for furniture recycling
product_ecotaxes_pmcb_description: Since January 1, 2023, all building construction products and materials have been subject to PMCB eco-participation. The eco-participation is indicated on the labels visibly and separately from the price of the product. This helps finance the collection, recycling and decontamination of these products. Its amount varies depending on the product and the type of treatment it requires.
